item.title: Govt to break oligarchic grip on energy sector: PM Finance Adviser
item.description: PM Finance Adviser criticised the previous government’s energy policies, saying they had created a structurally flawed system benefiting a small group of influential interests.

item.title: Health minister for public awareness to combat measles, dengue
item.description: Sakhawat Hossain said that if breastfeeding, nutritious food, cleanliness, isolation, and timely vaccination can be ensured, both measles and dengue can be largely brought under control.

item.title: Government working to prevent road accidents: Minister
item.description: The minister said that public unawareness and the operation of different types of small and large vehicles on the same roads are also among the major causes of accidents.

item.title: Govt encouraging expansion of export capacity, says state minister
item.description: The state minister said the government is prioritising earning foreign currency through the export of domestically produced goods and farm-based products, while ensuring local demand is met.

item.title: Speaker, French envoy discuss bilateral ties, trade cooperation
item.description: During the meeting, both sides exchanged views on strengthening people-to-people contacts, enhancing inter-parliamentary relations, women’s political empowerment, human rights and the role of Bangladeshi peacekeepers deployed in United Nations peacekeeping missions.

item.title: PM meets Editors’ Council leaders at secretariat
item.description: Prime Minister Tarique Rahman, alongside senior ministers and advisers, met Editors’ Council leaders at the secretariat before joining them for a luncheon.

item.title: Ukraine drones kill at least four in Russia, Moscow faces biggest attack in over a year
item.description: Ukraine has stepped up drone attacks on targets deep inside Russia in recent weeks, aiming to knock out oil refineries, depots and pipelines, as both sides seek to degrade each other’s infrastructure.

item.title: Additional Secretary Abdul Mannan appointed as Petrobangla chairman
item.description: Md Abdul Mannan, who serves in the planning sub-division of the Energy and Mineral Resources Division, was handed the additional charge of chairman on Sunday by a routine office order.

item.title: Student clash over DUET vice-chancellor leaves 20 injured
item.description: Tensions flared when the protesting group padlocked the main gate and the pro-appointment side attempted to force it open.

item.title: Hajj 2026: 60,991 Bangladeshi pilgrims reach Saudi Arabia so far
item.description: A total of 78,500 people from Bangladesh have been allowed to perform Hajj this year.

item.title: Minister says roadmap set for leather sector’s short- and long-term development
item.description: Preservation capacity must be expanded, the industries minister said, to prevent losses and turn the sector into a significant income stream.

item.title: Afsana Begum reappointed as director of National Book Centre
item.description: In September 2025, the interim government appointed Afsana Begum as director of the National Book Centre for a two-year term. However, she was relieved of the position in January, before the completion of her tenure.

item.title: PM’s ICT adviser hopes Bangladesh joins top 20 digital economies within five years
item.description: Rehan Asif Asad, speaking at a Dhaka discussion marking World Telecommunication and Information Society Day, noted that the ambition was rooted in long-term international data.

item.title: PM attends funeral prayer of ex-State Minister Mizanur Rahman Sinha
item.description: After the Janaza, Prime Minister Tarique Rahman paid tribute to Mizanur Rahman Sinha by placing a wreath on the coffin of the deceased.

item.title: Mahasthangarh: an archaeological site on the verge of destruction; artefacts being stolen
item.description: The heritage is gradually fading due to neglect and poor maintenance. Many important structures are slowly disappearing due to a lack of regular preservation efforts. Waterlogging during the monsoon, the spread of weeds, and soil erosion have collectively placed the existence of the archaeological site under serious threat.

item.title: Government borrows BDT 460 billion through treasury bills in April
item.description: Bangladesh Bank data show gross treasury bill issuance totalled BDT 460 billion in April 2026. Of that, BDT 328 billion went to repaying maturing obligations, leaving net issuance at BDT 132 billion.

item.title: Putin to visit Chinese leader Xi Jinping days after Trump’s trip to Beijing
item.description: The announcement comes less than 24 hours after U.S. President Donald Trump finished his own state visit to China, where he also met Xi to discuss trade and the U.S. and Israel’s war in Iran.

item.title: Israeli strike kills Hamas’ military wing leader, who Israel says was an architect of Oct 7 attacks
item.description: Izz al-Din al-Haddad was killed on Friday, Israel’s army said, describing him as one of the senior Hamas military commanders who directed the planning and execution of the Hamas-led attacks on October 7, 2023.

item.title: Trump returns from China with stability and a stalemate
item.description: Two days of talks between Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping underscored that even after Trump’s “Liberation Day” tariffs and the ensuing trade detente the ‌two sides reached late last year, Washington and Beijing are still locked in the contest that Trump inherited when he started his second term.

item.title: Moon sighting committee meets Monday to fix Eid-ul-Adha date
item.description: The committee will assemble at 6:45 pm local time tomorrow in the meeting room of the Islamic Foundation at Baitul Mukarram.

item.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in Congo, Uganda an emergency of international concern
item.description: The WHO said the outbreak, caused by the Bundibugyo virus, does not meet the criteria of a pandemic emergency but that countries sharing land borders with DRC ​are at high risk for further spread.

item.title: Canada deepens Arctic defense ties with Nordics after Trump threats
item.description: As Canada attempts to pivot away from relying on the US to protect its vast Arctic, Prime Minister ​Mark Carney is strengthening ties and exchanging security tips with the Nordic countries, which he describes as trusted partners.

item.title: Trump says Xi agrees Iran must open strait, China says war should not have started
item.description: Flying back from Beijing on Friday after two days of talks with Xi, Trump said he was considering whether to lift U.S. sanctions on Chinese oil companies buying Iranian oil. China is the biggest buyer of Iranian oil.

item.title: Trump’s geopolitical brinkmanship has hit a wall with Iran
item.description: With the two sides deadlocked, Trump has signaled growing frustration over the 11-week-old crisis ​but has shown little inclination to soften his harsh diplomatic approach toward Iran’s leaders.

item.title: Farakka agreement signing must maintain Bangladesh’s interests, says LGRD minister
item.description: The minister said Bangladesh would not permit any agreement that compromised its interests.

item.title: BAU innovation sorts 500kg of potatoes an hour at minimal cost
item.description: The new device sorts more than 500 kg of potatoes per hour at a cost of just 12 poisha (BDT 0.12) per kilogram, making it roughly 70 times cheaper than manual grading, according to the research team’s calculations.

item.title: Govt to distribute 9,800 tonnes of salt for preserving sacrificial animal hides
item.description: The Bangladesh Small and Cottage Industries Corporation (BSCIC) will complete the initiative within the next week.

item.title: PM inaugurates Khordda Canal re-excavation in Chandpur
item.description: The prime minister arrived at the site by road around 2.40 pm and unveiled a plaque, then cut the soil with a spade to launch the work and planted saplings on the canal bank.

item.title: Island district Bhola’s enduring role in national politics over six decades
item.description: After Bangladesh’s independence, the district remained a deeply active and consequential force in national politics. Several national-level figures have risen from Bhola, and they have helped chart the country’s political course — from both inside and outside the corridors of power.

item.title: JU academic exhibition challenges Johari Window theory
item.description: Associate Professor Salma Ahmed and students of the department’s 53rd batch jointly organised the event as part of the JMS 202: Interpersonal and Group Communication course.

item.title: Bangladesh, Angola launch energy partnership talks
item.description: The landmark virtual meeting, held at Bangladesh’s initiative on Friday, brought together senior government officials and state energy entities from both countries.

item.title: Hajj 2026: 59,055 Bangladeshi pilgrims reach Saudi Arabia so far
item.description: A total of 660 agencies are handling Hajj operations under private management, including 30 lead agencies and 630 coordinating agencies.

item.title: Bangladesh, Brazil eye strategic partnership, deeper cooperation
item.description: Both sides underscored the need to transform the existing friendly relations into a strategic partnership, reflecting the growing convergence between Bangladesh and Brazil as emerging voices of the Global South.

item.title: BAJUS cuts gold, silver prices
item.description: Under the revised rates, 22-carat gold will sell at BDT 238,121 per bhori (11.664 gram) in the local market.

item.title: Power Grid Bangladesh sinks deeper into loss as debt nears BDT 600 billion
item.description: Senior executives say transmission volumes have undershot targets, creating a revenue shortfall that feeds directly into the losses. The utility must shoulder heavy operating costs even though much of its newly built infrastructure sits underused.

item.title: US government planning dramatic Colorado River water cuts due to drought, overuse
item.description: The maximum possible federal cuts, ​which would be reviewed every two years based on water levels, are nearly twice as large as ​a May 1 offer by those three lower-basin states to reduce their water use by 1.6 million acre-feet per year.

item.title: SpaceX accelerates IPO timeline, targets June 12 listing on Nasdaq, sources say
item.description: SpaceX’s debut comes at ​a pivotal moment for the IPO market, which has rebounded after struggling over the past couple of years amid volatility ​fueled by U.S. tariff policy and geopolitical uncertainty.

item.title: US plans to indict Raul Castro raise Cubans’ fears of force
item.description: An indictment of the 94-year-old revolutionary icon would mark a major escalation in the pressure campaign against Cuba by the Trump administration.

item.title: Pageantry over policy: Takeaways from Trump’s China trip
item.description: Trump ended his trip without any obvious sign of securing China’s help, and it remains unclear how hard China is willing to push for Iran to do a deal or what it would want in return for its help.

item.title: Mizanur Rahman Sinha, former state minister, dies at 83
item.description: Mizanur Rahman Sinha served as state minister for health and family welfare from 2003 to 2006 and won the Munshiganj-2 parliamentary seat on the BNP nomination in the 1996 and 2001 general elections.

item.title: Iran has ‘no trust’ in US, will negotiate only if it is serious, Araqchi says
item.description: Araqchi told reporters in New Delhi that all vessels can pass through the Strait of ​Hormuz except those “at war” with Tehran, if they coordinate with Iran’s navy.

item.title: Trump leaves Beijing with few wins but warm words for Xi
item.description: Trump’s visit to America’s ​main strategic and economic rival, the first by a U.S. president since his last trip in 2017, aimed for tangible results to beef up his sagging approval ratings before the midterm elections.

item.title: Canal excavation programme will bring positive changes to rural economy: Minister
item.description: The minister said that using canal water would increase irrigation facilities, drainage systems would help eliminate waterlogging, and opportunities would be created for tree plantation and fish farming along canal banks.

item.title: Sports key to keeping children away from drugs: Energy Minister
item.description: The minister urged ensuring at least two hours of daily sports activities for children and adolescents to help them grow up in a healthy and sound environment.

item.title: Bangladesh’s foreign currency reserve stands at $34.32 billion
item.description: Officials noted that the reserve position reflects the country’s external sector stability amidst ongoing global economic uncertainties.

item.title: Bangladesh, US seal landmark energy cooperation pact
item.description: The MoU would support Bangladesh’s efforts to strengthen long-term energy security by diversifying its energy sourcing options based on affordability and supply chain sustainability.

item.title: Measles and measles-like symptoms claim 12 more children
item.description: DGHS Health Emergency Operation Center & Control Room disclosed the information on Friday afternoon.

item.title: Padma River’s course will change further over the next decade
item.description: Researchers stated that climate change and Padma Bridge’s impact will play major roles in this transformation, adding that the rapidly changing course of the river could significantly affect Bangladesh’s geography, public life, and economy.

item.title: Iran and UAE clash at BRICS foreign ministers’ meeting
item.description: ⁠The Iranian foreign minister stated that the truth is that the UAE was directly involved in the aggression ​against his country, adding that when the attacks started, they didn’t even issue a condemnation.

item.title: Boeing shares drop 4% after Trump announces China orders just 200 jets
item.description: A large Boeing order was one of many ⁠business deals expected to come out of the closely watched summit, as well as an extension of a fragile trade truce struck last October, ​when Trump suspended triple-digit tariffs on Chinese goods, and Xi backed away from choking global supplies of vital rare earths.

item.title: Govt to introduce special welfare card for cultural activists: Cultural Minister
item.description: The cultural minister said the government is taking effective measures to preserve the country’s archaeological sites, folk heritage, and historical landmarks, adding that protecting heritage means safeguarding the roots of the nation.

item.title: Shama Obaed Islam reaffirms peacekeeping, multilateral commitments at UN
item.description: During the meeting, the state minister outlined the key priorities of Bangladesh’s newly elected government, emphasising the restoration of public trust, strengthening democratic institutions, enhancing transparency and accountability, improving public service delivery, and upholding human rights and the rule of law.

item.title: Gold prices reduced, per bhori now at BDT 242,000
item.description: The new prices came into effect at 10 am on the same day.

item.title: FM joins UNGA presidency dialogue process
item.description: President of the 80th session of the General Assembly Annalena Baerbock convened the informal interactive dialogues with candidates for the presidency of the 81st UN session on Wednesday, which was broadcast live on UN WebTV.

item.title: Ensure proper use of public money: LGRD Minister
item.description: The minister said public welfare is the main objective of the government’s development activities, and all concerned must attach the highest importance to ensuring that not a single penny of public money is wasted.

item.title: Government issues notification declaring 7-day holiday for Eid-ul-Adha
item.description: All offices will remain closed for seven consecutive days from May 25 to May 31 for Eid-ul-Adha.

item.title: Majority of measles deaths among infants under 9 months
item.description: Despite the launch of special vaccination campaigns, the situation has not improved significantly. The concern is that infants below nine months of age, those still too young to receive the vaccine, are accounting for the highest number of deaths.

item.title: EC publishes updated electoral roll with over 128 million voters
item.description: The commission attributed the 0.5 percent increase to the enrolment of applicants born on or before January 1, 2008.

item.title: Labour minister orders all factories to pay Eid bonuses by May 21
item.description: The minister said local administrations, district police and industrial police had been placed on special alert ahead of the Eid period.

item.title: ‘Actuary’ in insurance industry and its importance, current status in Bangladesh
item.description: Actuarial science is a highly respected profession and offers both prestige and substantial financial rewards. Unfortunately, in Bangladesh, the actuarial profession has not yet developed adequately.

item.title: SSC exams to begin on January 7, HSC on June 6: Edu Minister
item.description: Education Minister ANM Ehsanul Hoque Milon announced the dates on Thursday at a press conference.

item.title: AI boom puts SK Hynix on cusp of $1 trillion market value
item.description: Valued at less than $100 ​billion just 16 months ago, SK Hynix’s market capitalisation stood at roughly $942 billion at the ⁠close of trading on Thursday.

item.title: EU ambassador calls on opposition leader
item.description: Their discussions focused on making democracy more meaningful after the national parliamentary election, ongoing reform initiatives and the implementation of referendum verdicts.

item.title: Govt plans land and tax incentives for private solar investment, minister says
item.description: The government wanted solar investment to match the scale of the country’s garments industry, the power minister said, arguing that upfront concessions would later generate revenue for the state.

item.title: Repayment pressure, no alternative jobs leave many farmers considering migration
item.description: For the farming communities of the haor region, migration has become a last refuge — a desperate move toward the cities in search of survival. But this path is not open to everyone.

item.title: Janata Bank’s capital shortfall hits BDT 644 billion as bad loans surge
item.description: Virtually all of Janata Bank’s large loans have soured, and it lacks adequate collateral against them. Attempts to sell pledged assets have also drawn no buyers. That has put two-thirds of its loan book at severe risk.

item.title: UAE denies Netanyahu secretly visited the country during the Iran war
item.description: The UAE’s official WAM news agency later posted an article denying “reports circulating” about a Netanyahu visit.

item.title: Russia pounds Ukraine in massive air attack
item.description: Kyiv-area officials reported ​one person killed and several dozen wounded in the ⁠overnight strike, which Prime Minister Yuliia Svyrydenko said targeted civilian infrastructure and ​residential buildings.

item.title: Bangladesh bets on Padma Barrage to revive rivers, boost farming
item.description: Officials estimate the barrage will store about 2,900 million cubic metres of water in the Padma, sustaining flow in roughly 623 small and large rivers across the region during the dry months.

item.title: Fitch cuts Bangladesh outlook to ‘negative’
item.description: The ratings agency ​warned Bangladesh faced significant downside risks through higher energy import ​costs and potential ​disruptions to remittances.

item.title: Indian rupee hits record low as worries over energy risks deepen
item.description: The ​rupee has declined 1.4 percent this week and ‌hit ⁠record lows in each trading session between Tuesday and Thursday.

item.title: UK’s Starmer faces biggest challenge yet as resignation threat overshadows King’s Speech
item.description: As Starmer and his ministers stood in silence to hear King Charles read out that agenda, the ceremony was overshadowed by what could be the most dangerous threat to the prime minister.

item.title: Protests flare across Havana as power cuts deepen amid US blockade
item.description: Cuba’s energy and mines minister earlier ​in the day said the nation had completely run ​out of diesel and fuel oil, and that its power grid had entered ‌a “critical” ⁠state.

item.title: Iran war overshadows Trump’s China visit as peace talks stall
item.description: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran looms large over Trump’s visit ‌to China, the first by a U.S. president since his last trip there in 2017.

item.title: China’s Xi signals trade progress as ‘biggest summit’ with Trump begins
item.description: Xi opened the summit by telling Trump that stable China–U.S. relationship benefits the entire world.

item.title: Trump lands in China for Xi summit with Nvidia CEO in tow
item.description: Trump is seeking to snag some economic wins on the ‌first visit by a U.S. president to China in nearly a decade and maintain a fragile trade truce to prop up public approval ratings bruised by his war with Iran.

item.title: PM directs authorities to ensure rights, services for persons with disabilities
item.description: The prime minister emphasised the importance of establishing disability-friendly infrastructure and ensuring access to all forms of facilities, including education and healthcare, to build an inclusive society.

item.title: Govt-private initiatives are vital to boost livestock sector: State Minister
item.description: The state minister stated that the importance of livestock and biodiversity is immense in maintaining environmental balance and ensuring food security for the people.

item.title: Sinovac Foundation donates 383,084 doses of polio vaccine to Bangladesh
item.description: The Foundation donated the vaccine to make the ongoing activities of the Bangladesh government’s Expanded Programme on Immunisation (EPI) more rapid, dynamic, and effective.

item.title: Social stability key to economic revival: Info Minister
item.description: The minister said social and political stability at the national level is crucial for the development and prosperity of the country during this critical time.

item.title: Measles vaccine reaches 95% of children, says DGHS chief
item.description: The health directorate added that there is currently no vaccine shortage in the country and that children who are yet to be immunised would be vaccinated swiftly.

item.title: Govt raises rawhide prices ahead of Eid-ul-Adha
item.description: The rate marks a modest increase from BDT 60–65 last year. Outside the capital, cow hides were fixed at BDT 57–62, up from the previous year’s BDT 55–60.

item.title: Measles and related symptoms claim eight more children in 24 hours
item.description: Confirmed measles claimed one more life in the last 24 hours, bringing the total number of confirmed measles-related deaths to 69 during the same period.

item.title: Forest guards rescue four kidnapped fishermen in Sundarbans after gunfight with bandits
item.description: A patrol from the Shelarchar outpost and a specialist smart team detected the bandits, who opened fire first. The forest guards returned seven rounds.

item.title: ECNEC approves 9 projects worth over BDT 366 billion, including Padma Barrage
item.description: Among the approved projects, three are new, five are revised, and one project got an extension of tenure without witnessing cost escalation.

item.title: Exploring opportunities and challenges to improving Bangladesh–Pakistan relationship
item.description: The Pakistan-Bangladesh relationship in 2026 is no longer defined by the past, but by pragmatic necessity.

item.title: Nearly 50 factories may face crisis over wages, bonuses ahead of Eid-ul-Adha
item.description: Industrial Police said they are maintaining regular communication with factory owners and trying to resolve the situation.

item.title: Rail engine shortage, damaged roads threaten Eid travel
item.description: Nearly 15 million people will travel from Dhaka to different destinations during the upcoming Eid holidays. In addition, nearly 10 million sacrificial animals will be transported across the country.

item.title: Import-heavy gas policy proved self-defeating
item.description: The country began importing LNG in 2018 to ease a chronic domestic supply crunch. Seven years on, the import bill has more than quintupled. Energy experts and economists now regard the gas sector’s master plan as a self-defeating decision.

item.title: OpenAI chief Sam Altman denies betraying Elon Musk, defends for-profit push at trial
item.description: In an August 2024 lawsuit, Musk accused Altman and OpenAI of persuading him into giving $38 million, only to see the ‌nonprofit abandon its mission to benefit humanity and instead become a for-profit corporation.

item.title: Trump says stopping Iran’s nuclear program outweighs Americans’ economic pain
item.description: Trump is under growing ​pressure from fellow Republicans who fear economic pain caused by the war could spark a backlash against the party and ‌cost ⁠it control of the House of Representatives and possibly the Senate in November.

item.title: Bangladesh’s foreign debt repayments double to more than $4 billion
item.description: The repayment trajectory has been consistently upward over the five years, indicating mounting pressure from foreign liabilities and the repayment schedule.

item.title: Trump’s FDA chief is out after angering pharma CEOs, vaping lobbyists and anti-abortion activists
item.description: A surgeon and health researcher, Makary came to prominence among Republicans as an outspoken critic of public health measures during the COVID-19 pandemic.

item.title: Nvidia CEO joins Trump’s mission to ‘open up’ China
item.description: The CEOs accompanying Trump are drawn mainly from companies seeking to resolve business issues with China, such as Nvidia.

item.title: Saudi Arabia launched covert attacks on Iran as regional war widened, sources say
item.description: The Saudi attacks, not previously reported, mark the first time that the ​kingdom is known to have directly carried out military action on Iranian soil and show it is becoming much bolder in defending itself against its main regional rival.

item.title: RSC grants safety recognition to 46 RMG factories
item.description: The additions mark continued progress in the industry’s safety overhaul, set in motion after the fatal Rana Plaza factory collapse in April 2013.

item.title: BGB remains fully alert to prevent ‘push-ins’ through the border: Home Minister
item.description: The home minister said Bangladesh maintains relations with friendly countries on the basis of sovereign equality.

item.title: PM seeks OIC support to resolve Rohingya crisis
item.description: The OIC member countries also expressed their strong commitment to standing beside Bangladesh and strengthening bilateral cooperation in various areas, including investment, trade, education, healthcare, textiles, and the pharmaceutical industries.

item.title: Bangladesh secure ‘hat-trick’ Test win over Pakistan
item.description: With the victory, the side led by Najmul Hossain Shanto took a 1-0 lead in the two-match Test series.

item.title: Home Minister unveils nationwide Eid security, monitoring measures
item.description: The minister said that the government has taken wide-ranging decisions to ensure public safety and smooth Eid celebrations across the country.

item.title: Nearly half of Bangladeshi graduates do not use computers
item.description: Digital connectivity is spreading, yet computer-based competence is not following among Bangladeshi graduates — and that deficit is now a serious obstacle to entering professional workplaces and participating in higher-end digital activity.

item.title: Fund launched to enhance local disaster response capacity
item.description: The minister said the joint fund, formed by utilising the experience, institutional capacity, and grassroots engagement of local NGOs and microfinance institutions, would open a new horizon in humanitarian aid management.

item.title: Govt to procure 95 million doses of vaccines: PM’s Info Adviser
item.description: The adviser said that the move aims to strengthen the ongoing vaccination campaign across the country.

item.title: Nine more children die from measles and measles-like symptoms in 24 hours
item.description: In total, 68 people have died from confirmed measles infections since March 15, according to DGHS data.

item.title: Economy may take at least two years to stabilise, says finance minister
item.description: The minister stated that ensuring energy security and reducing corruption through the digitalisation of citizen services are currently among the government’s top priorities.

item.title: Germany must pull itself together, Merz tells unions as jeers ring out
item.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz said high costs and bureaucracy were hurting business, putting jobs and the prosperity of future generations ⁠at risk.

item.title: Bangladesh condemns killing of two nationals in Lebanon
item.description: The foreign ministry described the attack as “heinous”, expressing its profound grief and offering its deepest condolences to the families.

item.title: Finance minister rules out political appointees to financial sector
item.description: The minister acknowledged persistent strains across the financial system but insisted recovery was underway. “It may be taking some time. But we will recover,” he said.

item.title: PM calls for focusing on research, innovation in universities
item.description: Noting that research publications, citations and innovation are given significant importance in global rankings, the premier said educationists must think more deeply about where Bangladesh stand in these areas.

item.title: Beyond Barapukuria, Bangladesh still undecided on mining four major coalfields
item.description: Successive governments shied away from authorising extraction at four major coal fields. Import dependence consequently deepened, leaving the vast resource stranded underground.

item.title: Govt eyes making technical, practical education compulsory from school, PM says
item.description: The prime minister also flagged a growing tendency for graduates to remain unemployed and said the government was updating syllabi to close the gap.

item.title: US says 18 passengers flown back after hantavirus outbreak on ship
item.description: The group had been aboard the MV Hondius, a luxury ​expedition cruise ship linked to an outbreak of the Andes virus, the only hantavirus species known to be capable ​of limited spread from person-to-person.

item.title: US issues new sanctions over Iran’s oil shipments to China
item.description: The U.S. Treasury move follows sanctions announced on Friday on individuals and companies aiding Iranian purchases of weapons and components used to make drones and ballistic missiles.

item.title: Peace deal hopes fade after Trump rejects ‘garbage’ Iran proposal
item.description: Trump said Iran’s response threatened the status of ​a ceasefire that began on April 7.

item.title: Stung by Iran war, Trump heads to China in need of wins
item.description: The modest expectations for Trump’s May 14-15 meetings with Xi Jinping underscore how Trump’s bombastic approach has failed to deliver an advantage ahead of the talks, according to analysts.

item.title: Finance minister flags tax-to-GDP sliding below 7% as ‘alarming’
item.description: Any future investment or project approval would have to satisfy four criteria, the minister announced: guaranteed returns, job creation, environmental protection and measurable social impact.

item.title: Digital tax structure can reduce gap between revenue targets and collection: Ahteshamul Haque Khan
item.description: Md Ahteshamul Haque Khan called for determining Bangladesh’s future economic roadmap by utilising the country’s capabilities through industry- and business-friendly policy support.

item.title: ‘Sonar Bangla Policy Conclave’ on Bangladesh’s economic future opens in Dhaka
item.description: The event, organised by The Daily Bonik Barta, began at 7 pm at the Grand Ballroom of Sheraton Hotel under the theme “Shaping Bangladesh’s Economic Future during Global Uncertainty”.

item.title: Alihat mine left idle after iron ore reserves deemed unextractable
item.description: Experts note that even when initial assessments prove unpromising, broader surveys and research can still yield better results. Mineral-rich countries routinely undertake such exhaustive programmes; Bangladesh rarely does.

item.title: Asia braces for a second wave of energy shocks from the Iran war
item.description: About 8.8 million people are in danger of being pushed into poverty and the conflict may cause $299 billion in economic losses to the Asia-Pacific region, according to the United Nations Development Program.

item.title: Three crude oil tankers exit Strait of Hormuz with trackers switched off, data shows
item.description: Two very large crude carriers (VLCC), the Agios Fanourios I and the Kiara M, ​carrying 2 million barrels of Iraqi crude each, passed through the strait ⁠on Sunday, the data showed.

item.title: State minister urges skill development for promoting safe, orderly migration
item.description: The state minister emphasised the “zero tolerance” commitment of the new government led by the Hon’ble Prime Minister Tarique Rahman toward illegal migration.

item.title: Padma barrage project worth around BDT 335 billion likely to go before ECNEC
item.description: Once implemented, the project is expected to revive around 623 small and large rivers across the region and improve water availability.

item.title: Coordinated enforcement curbs poaching at Sundarbans, wildlife begins to recover
item.description: The effect on the ecosystem is now visible. Fishermen and honey collectors who have worked the forest for up to 15 years report encountering tiger pugmarks and hearing tiger calls with a frequency absent for more than a decade.

item.title: No position in police administration is permanent: Prime Minister
item.description: The prime minister said that just as government isn’t permanent in a democratic system, no administrative post remains reserved for anyone indefinitely.

item.title: Govt to build strategic reserves for ‘select essentials’, says commerce minister
item.description: The minister noted the government wants a partnership-based framework to open the market to more businesses and intensify competition.

item.title: Greece’s banking recovery offers possible model for Bangladesh
item.description: Bangladesh does not yet face insolvency on the Greek scale. But for years its economy has been caught in sluggish GDP growth, below-target revenue collection, climbing government debt and interest costs, high inflation, and persistent unemployment.

item.title: Netanyahu wants to wean Israel off US military support, he tells CBS
item.description: Netanyahu said Israel ​receives about $3.8 billion of U.S. military aid a year. The U.S. has agreed to provide a total of $38 billion ​in military aid to Israel from 2018 to 2028.

item.title: Thailand’s former PM Thaksin freed from prison
item.description: The 76-year-old tycoon remade and dominated Thai politics for a quarter-century, but his influence has waned of late.

item.title: One US citizen tests mildly positive for hantavirus, another has mild symptoms
item.description: Hantaviruses ⁠are a group of viruses that are usually spread by rodents but ​in rare cases can be transmitted person to person.

item.title: Trump and China’s Xi set for talks spanning Iran, nuclear, trade and AI
item.description: Trump is scheduled to arrive in Beijing on Wednesday, ahead of talks set to take place Thursday and Friday. It will be his first trip to China since 2017.

item.title: HC bans disclosure of unborn child’s sex, citing rights violations
item.description: The court held that sex determination and disclosure of an unborn child breached women’s constitutional rights to dignity, equality and life, undermined social equilibrium and violated international human rights obligations.

item.title: TCB to sell subsidised oil, sugar and lentils ahead of Eid
item.description: The 10-day programme runs until May 21, with no sales on Fridays, and will supply to roughly 2.88 million people.

item.title: Trump rejects Iran’s response to US peace proposal as ‘unacceptable’
item.description: Days ‌after the U.S. floated an offer in the hopes of re-opening negotiations, Iran on Sunday released a response focused on ending the war on all fronts. Within ​hours, Trump dismissed Iran’s proposal with a post on social media.

item.title: Iran says it has sent response to US peace proposal
item.description: After some 48 hours of relative calm following sporadic clashes last week, hostile drones were detected over several Gulf countries on Sunday, underlining the threat still facing the region despite a month-old ceasefire.

item.title: Aramco Q1 profit jumps 25 percent as Hormuz risks push pipeline to full capacity
item.description: Total revenue surged nearly 7 percent from a year earlier to $115.49 billion ​due to higher prices and volumes sold of both crude oil and refined and ⁠chemical products.

item.title: Info minister for grooming youth with moral education, digital literacy
item.description: The minister said that emphasis should be placed on promoting content on social media that helps create value among the youth.

item.title: Tanker crosses Strait of Hormuz as US waits for Iran’s response to peace proposal
item.description: The United States is still waiting for a response from Tehran to a proposal that would formally end the war before the start ​of talks on more contentious issues, including Iran’s nuclear program.

item.title: Bangladesh, Italy urge stronger cooperation on safe migration
item.description: The meeting, described as fruitful, explored ways to strengthen long-standing friendly relations and addressed issues of mutual interest.

item.title: Mutual trust between people and police to make controlling crime easier, says PM
item.description: If the police can become a symbol of trust and security for the people, that is the success of the police, the prime minister said, adding that the success of the police means the success of the government as well.

item.title: Health and education allocations double; power and energy share decline
item.description: In terms of allocation, priority has been given to commitments outlined in the government’s election manifesto.

item.title: Govt would meet police force’s rational demands, says home minister
item.description: The minister added that the government would grant honorary promotions upon retirement to officers with satisfactory service records.

item.title: ICT-1 indicts Nanak, Taposh, 26 others over Mohammadpur killings
item.description: The court also set June 8 for the prosecution’s opening statement and to start recording the deposition of the witnesses.

item.title: How China’s evolving consumer habits may protect the Amazon rainforest
item.description: The pledge challenges a long-held assumption among Brazilian farmers: that China, the world’s largest importer of beef and soy, cares only about price.

item.title: Spanish health officials board hantavirus-hit ship to start evacuating passengers
item.description: The passengers will board ​a flight back to Madrid on a Spanish military plane, government officials said, emphasising that they ⁠will have no contact with members of the public.

item.title: Finance minister stresses inclusive economy for wider participation
item.description: Highlighting the importance of utilising the country’s demographic dividend, the finance minister said the remaining window of opportunity must be fully harnessed through skill development, employment and entrepreneurship programmes.

item.title: Central bank sold $35.35 billion in five years, bought $12.33 billion
item.description: Bangladesh Bank made a net profit of BDT 1.58 trillion over the past five fiscal years, its own data show. Most of it derived from foreign exchange trading.

item.title: Car bombing and shootout kills at least 14 Pakistani police officers
item.description: Police official Sajjad Khan said in a statement that ⁠the bodies of 14 officers had been recovered from the collapsed outpost and ​three other personnel were found alive and rushed to hospital.

item.title: Prime minister warns against misuse of police against citizens
item.description: The prime minister added that people who had long faced repression, including repeated attacks and legal cases, now sought peace and security under a democratic government.

item.title: Why oil extraction stopped at Haripur field remains unclear even after three decades
item.description: Energy sector insiders note that, for reasons nobody has explained, no modern extraction technique was ever applied to the oil deposits at Haripur.

item.title: Award ceremony postponed ahead of Police Week 2026
item.description: Officials confirmed that all other Police Week 2026 events will proceed as scheduled.

item.title: Putin says he thinks Russia-Ukraine war is coming to an end
item.description: Russian troops have been fighting ​in Ukraine for well over four years. That is longer than Soviet forces fought in World War Two, known in Russia as the Great Patriotic War of 1941-45.

item.title: Why millions of Americans pay for unfinished electricity projects
item.description: At least 40 US states now have some form of CWIP incentive, according to a Reuters review of several thousand pages of electric utility rate disclosures.

item.title: UK deploys warship to Middle East with eye on potential Hormuz mission
item.description: HMS Dragon, an air defence destroyer, was sent to the Eastern Mediterranean in March, ​shortly after the start of the Iran war, to help ​defend Cyprus.

item.title: ‘Study in Malaysia’ expo underway in Dhaka amid strong student interest
item.description: The expo runs at the Sarina Hotel until May 9, then moves to The Grand Riverview Hotel in Rajshahi on May 13 and The Peninsula Hotel Chittagong on May 15.

item.title: US, Iran no closer to ending war as Gulf clashes flare
item.description: Tehran has ​largely blocked non-Iranian shipping through the strait since the war began with U.S.-Israeli airstrikes across Iran on February 28. Before the war, one-fifth of the world’s oil supply passed through the narrow waterway.

item.title: Traffic at Karnaphuli Tunnel stays far below forecast even during holiday rush
item.description: Built under the Karnaphuli River with Chinese loans, the tunnel was projected to handle 17,000 vehicles a day from the outset. It initially managed between 4,000 and 5,000. That figure has since sunk to 3,000–3,500.

item.title: PM seeks BNP grassroots leaders’ help in implementing election pledges
item.description: The prime minister said, “One of our battles has ended. Now another battle has begun. That is to implement the commitments we made to the people”.

item.title: Govt determined to reopen state-owned sugar mills, says industries minister
item.description: The minister added that modernisation, renovation and the adoption of new technology were prerequisites for any meaningful revival of the country’s closed state-owned sugar mills.

item.title: Bangladesh’s Education Paradox: More girls in class, fewer women in charge
item.description: Education stakeholders demand specific field-level research and effective action to determine why women are falling behind, which barriers most obstruct their progress and which existing obstacles create the deepest challenges.

item.title: State minister for foreign affairs meets visiting interior minister of Pakistan
item.description: The two sides discussed various issues of bilateral interest and reaffirmed their commitment to strengthening bilateral engagement and cooperation.

item.title: Law minister urges newly appointed judges to deliver verdicts responsibly
item.description: The minister also urged judges to avoid any factor that might obstruct the delivery of justice.

item.title: Tarique Rahman joins views-exchange meeting with BNP district-level leaders
item.description: The day-long meeting began at 10:45 am at the Krishibid Institution Bangladesh (KIB) auditorium in the city’s Farmgate area with BNP Senior Joint Secretary General Ruhul Kabir Rizvi in the chair.

item.title: One operational vehicle for every 40 police personnel in Bangladesh
item.description: Sources said many police stations rely on old vehicles that spend much of their time out of service, further hampering crime suppression, law enforcement and citizen services.

item.title: What we don’t know about the hantavirus outbreak as the cruise ship nears Spanish territory
item.description: The vessel is expected to reach the Spanish island of Tenerife, off the coast of West Africa, early Sunday.

item.title: US concerned by Taiwan defence delay ‘concession’ to China
item.description: The U.S. is Taiwan’s most important international backer and arms supplier, despite the lack of formal ‌diplomatic ⁠ties, and has strongly supported increased military spending. Beijing has repeatedly demanded an end to weapons sales.

item.title: World Bank warns Bangladesh’s self-employed remain overwhelmingly low-skilled
item.description: Economists caution that while self-employment contributes substantially to the economy, the preponderance of low skills could erode Bangladesh’s long-term competitiveness.

item.title: Russia holds scaled-back WW2 victory parade as worries over war in Ukraine deepen
item.description: Once used to show off Russia’s vast military, the parade this year will have no tanks or other military equipment rolling over the cobbles of Red Square.

item.title: Trump’s feuds, tensions with allies likely to outlast Iran war
item.description: Tensions are especially high between Trump and the Europeans since he joined Israel in striking Iran on February 28, claiming without evidence that Tehran was close to developing a nuclear weapon.

item.title: Tehran could withstand blockade for four months, CIA report shows, as fighting flares
item.description: A CIA assessment indicated that Iran would not ‌suffer severe economic pressure from a U.S. blockade of Iranian ports for about another four months, according to a U.S. official familiar with the matter.

item.title: Dhaka receives remains of second student murdered in Florida
item.description: Bristy, 27, was a PhD candidate in chemical engineering at the University of South Florida. She and Zamil Ahamed Limon, also 27, disappeared on April 16.

item.title: Govt planning five-year programme for flood-prone Haor region: PM’s Adviser
item.description: The government has already begun framing an integrated plan to produce sustainable solutions, said the adviser.

item.title: Bangladesh, Pakistan sign MoU to combat drug trafficking, narcotics abuse
item.description: According to the agreement, Bangladesh and Pakistan will regularly exchange information on drug traffickers, drug trafficking organisations (DTOs), emerging smuggling routes, and new concealment techniques used in narcotics trafficking.

item.title: Old vehicles worsening traffic congestion in Dhaka
item.description: To restore discipline on Dhaka’s roads and reduce public suffering, the DMP has taken a strict stance, announcing special drives against dilapidated, risky, and unfit vehicles that have exceeded their designated economic lifespan.

item.title: Indian High Commission celebrated Rabindra Jayanti, inaugurating art exhibition
item.description: Inaugurating the exhibition, High Commissioner Pranay Verma described the shared artistic and cultural traditions between India and Bangladesh as the bedrock of their relationship.

item.title: Multinational companies contribute less than 5% to Bangladesh’s GDP
item.description: In both cases — Bangladesh’s GDP and exports, the participation of MNEs in 2020 remained at the same level as in 2009.

item.title: Water pressure eases in haor region, but several rivers remain above danger level
item.description: Several rivers in the districts of Sunamganj, Netrokona, and Habiganj are still flowing above the pre-monsoon danger level. However, water levels have started to decline gradually in many areas.

item.title: Bangladesh stresses stronger global cooperation to ensure migrants’ rights
item.description: The minister said Bangladesh was strengthening accountability and expanding migrants’ access to justice through a “whole-of-government and whole-of-society” approach.

item.title: Russia’s internet crackdown hobbles small businesses
item.description: Restrictions on popular messenger apps such as Telegram, curbs on ‌VPNs, and security-linked mobile internet shutdowns have affected much of Russia this year, but the unpredictable outages pose a particular headache for many small companies, with billions of dollars in digital sales at risk.

item.title: UK’s Labour Party punished in local elections, populist Reform gains
item.description: The main beneficiary was the populist Reform UK party of Brexit campaigner Nigel Farage, which gained more than 300 council seats in England, and could form the main opposition in Scotland and Wales to the pro-independence Scottish National Party and Plaid Cymru.

item.title: Trump says ceasefire still holds after fighting between the US and Iran flares
item.description: The flare-up in fighting came as Washington awaited a response from Tehran to its proposal to end the conflict, which began with joint U.S.-Israeli airstrikes across Iran on February 28.

item.title: Meta challenges UK media regulator over online safety fees
item.description: An Ofcom ​spokesperson said in a statement that fees and penalties ​under the law are based on a provider’s ⁠qualifying worldwide revenue.

item.title: BNP govt’s first budget session begins on June 7
item.description: Finance Minister Amir Khasru Mahmud Chowdhury is expected to present the first budget of the BNP government.

item.title: Salt prices rise ahead of Eid-ul-Adha, fears grow over lower free supply
item.description: Bangladesh’s salt production target for the current season was set at around 2.71 million tonnes. In the season that began in November, approximately 1.76 million tonnes had been collected as of April 30.

item.title: Minister urges wider use of modern water treatment technology
item.description: The rural development minister said the government was taking effective steps to curb pollution in the country’s rivers.

item.title: Dhaka and Beijing seek stronger ties across trade and regional affairs
item.description: The foreign minister highlighted Prime Minister Tarique Rahman’s vision of building a connected, economically vibrant and globally engaged Bangladesh.

item.title: Energy minister says govt preparing new policy push for solar, renewable sector
item.description: The minister said that officials were working to finalise the policy this month, with a view to issuing it as a government order after the Cabinet Division approves it in June.

item.title: Delayed investigations leave 52% of murder cases without convictions, PBI report finds
item.description: The PBI study further found that trial proceedings take many times longer than the investigation phase. In some instances, courts hand down judgments 30 years after a murder.

item.title: Higher auction price floor boosts tea estates’ earnings by BDT 4.65 billion
item.description: The sales revenue has increased after a minimum price floor was introduced at auctions for two consecutive years.

item.title: US and Iran inch towards short-term deal to end fighting
item.description: Tehran and Washington have scaled back ambitions for a sweeping settlement as differences persist, particularly over Iran's nuclear programme — including the fate of its highly enriched uranium stockpiles and how long ​Tehran would halt nuclear work.

item.title: Army to be withdrawn gradually from field-level law enforcement duties: Home Minister
item.description: The minister said the withdrawal process would be carried out in phases through coordination among the police, army and other relevant agencies.

item.title: Rural areas lag in govt education facilities with nearly 93% of institutions in urban areas
item.description: Education stakeholders believe that high costs and the lack of adequate government facilities in rural areas are the main reasons behind student dropouts.

item.title: “A national budget should serve as a policy document for economic transformation”
item.description: “The fundamental weakness is that we still treat the budget essentially as an income-expenditure ledger. A national budget should serve as a policy document for economic transformation. It should spell out which sectors the government will prioritise over the coming year…”

item.title: ADB warns crude prices to reach $96 a barrel this year
item.description: The Manila-based lender also slashed its growth projections for developing Asia and the Pacific as disrupted energy shipments continue to cascade through supply chains.

item.title: Fake rumors, real killings: Inside Congo’s deadly health misinformation crisis
item.description: Angry mobs attacked and killed four health workers conducting vaccination ​research in an episode that took place in October, a deadly example of the rising danger posed by online health misinformation in Africa.

item.title: BAJUS raises gold, silver prices again in domestic market
item.description: So far this year, BAJUS adjusted gold prices 63 times, raising them on 35 occasions and lowering them 28 times.

item.title: Bangladesh inflation climbs above 9% again
item.description: Analysts note that both food and non-food price rises stoked the April upswing. Transport costs climbed amid the fuel crunch, constricting food supplies. Non-food items such as building materials, electronics and automobiles also became costlier.

item.title: France moves aircraft carrier to Red Sea with eye on Hormuz mission
item.description: France and Britain have been working on a proposal for several weeks that aims to lay the groundwork for safe transit through the ​Strait once the situation stabilises or the conflict is resolved.

item.title: Trump sees swift end to war as Iran reviews US peace proposal
item.description: The two sides remain ⁠at odds over a variety of difficult issues, such as Iran’s nuclear ambitions and its control of the Strait of Hormuz, which before the war handled one-fifth of ​the world’s oil and gas supply.

item.title: “Upcoming budget will include every individual and community”
item.description: “In the past, many groups remained outside the scope of the budget, with no dedicated projects for them. This time, we will bring them under inclusion. We have spoken about the ’democratisation of the economy’ — this is our slogan.”

item.title: Overreliance on single sector is risky for Bangladesh’s economy
item.description: The ADB chief economist cautioned that excessive dependence on a single sector is risky, and any unexpected shock to that sector could deliver a major blow to the economy — something observed in the past.

item.title: BDT 740 billion proposed as block allocation in FY2026–27 development budget
item.description: Of the proposed development budget of BDT 3 trillion, BDT 2.26 trillion has been allocated for development projects, while the remaining BDT 740 billion will be kept as a block allocation for development assistance and social development support to meet special needs.

item.title: Bangladesh secures $1 billion budget support as finance minister meets ADB president
item.description: The ADB has also pledged around $1.4 billion in project assistance, which is also committed for the current fiscal year.

item.title: Trump pauses effort to escort ships in Strait of Hormuz, citing deal progress
item.description: Trump wrote on social media that the two sides have mutually agreed that, while the Blockade will remain in full force and effect, Project Freedom ... will be paused for a short period of time to see whether or not the Agreement can be finalised and signed.

item.title: Gold, silver prices rise in domestic market
item.description: So far this year, BAJUS adjusted gold prices 62 times, raising them on 34 occasions and lowering them 28 times.

item.title: Bangladesh-US trade agreement should be utilised for mutual benefits: Commerce Minister
item.description: The minister said that a ‘win-win’ situation is ensured by taking into account the interests of both sides, adding that there is no need for unnecessary concern over this agreement.

item.title: Healthcare is a fundamental human right, not a privilege: Dr Zubaida
item.description: Dr Zubaida Rahman added that the goal is to build a healthy, more productive, self-reliant, and sovereign nation.

item.title: Six more deaths from measles and its symptoms; 1,445 newly affected
item.description: In total, 317 children have died across the country from measles and its symptoms over the past one and a half months, according to DGHS.

item.title: Advance train ticket sales for Eid journeys to begin on May 13
item.description: Each passenger will be allowed to purchase tickets only once for both advance and return journeys, with a maximum of four tickets per transaction in each case.

item.title: Selling fishes from Kaptai Lake brings BDT 225 million in revenue
item.description: The highest revenue share is now coming from Kachki and Chapila fish, accounting for 75%.

item.title: Home minister urges stronger global support for Rohingya refugees
item.description: The home minister called on the United Nations and relevant agencies to keep the crisis firmly on the global agenda.

item.title: FM begins China visit to deepen ties
item.description: The three-day visit, scheduled for May 5-7, is being made at the invitation of Wang Yi, Member of the Political Bureau of the Communist Party of China Central Committee and Foreign Minister of China.

item.title: Local govt polls likely to begin by year-end: PM’s Adviser
item.description: The adviser said that once the electoral process starts, it may take between 10 months and a maximum of one year to complete all phases.

item.title: PM for conducting performance audits to ensure transparency in public expenditures
item.description: The prime minister highlighted the importance of performance audits to ensure transparency and accountability in spending public funds.

item.title: Growers fear losses as adverse weather threatens mango production
item.description: Drought, pest attacks, and recent storms, rain, and hail have caused significant shedding of young mangoes. The adverse weather forecast due to Super El Niño has compounded the situation.

item.title: Deaths of Sikder family members leave billions in bad loans unresolved
item.description: The late Zainul Haque Sikder was reportedly regarded as a father figure by the deposed prime minister, Sheikh Hasina. The family parlayed this political influence into sole control of the National Bank PLC, a private-sector lender.

item.title: No reason for concern over Bangladesh-US trade deal, says commerce adviser
item.description: The pact, the commerce minister stressed, contained scope for necessary adjustments and revisions, leaving no grounds for excessive alarm.

item.title: How an ocean cruise turned into a hantavirus nightmare
item.description: About 150 people remain stuck on the ship, which had been visiting ​some of the most remote places on Earth.

item.title: Middle East truce in doubt as US and Iran fight for control of Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Several merchant ships ​in the Gulf reported explosions or fires on Monday, and an oil port in the United Arab Emirates, which hosts a large U.S. military base, was set ablaze by Iranian missiles.

item.title: Dhaka urges expanded ADB support as energy costs and climate risk mount
item.description: The finance minister lauded ADB’s timely budget support but argued that “counter-cyclical financing” must continue if global risks escalate further.

item.title: Gold prices cut by BDT 2,158 for 22-carat
item.description: BAJUS set the price for 22-carat gold at BDT 240,337 per bhori, effective from 10 am, citing a decrease in local market prices of pure gold.

item.title: Railway spent nearly twice what they earned in nine months
item.description: Railways ministry data show the government set a revenue target of BDT 31.73 billion for the 2025–26 fiscal year. Actual collections lag far behind that figure.

item.title: Taiwan won’t give in to pressure, president says of Africa trip China tried to stop
item.description: China views democratically governed Taiwan as part of its territory with no right to state-to-state ties, a position Taiwan’s government strongly disputes.

item.title: Blast at fireworks factory in China’s Hunan kills 21, Xi calls for probe, state media says
item.description: Nearly 500 firefighters, rescuers and medical personnel attended the scene, according to the South China Morning Post.

item.title: US and Iran launch new attacks as they wrestle for control of Gulf waters
item.description: The fresh volleys of missiles and drones came after U.S. President Donald Trump launched a new effort to ‌get stranded tankers and other ships through the strait.

item.title: Onion, garlic prices fall in Hili, ginger remains stable
item.description: Traders said the prices of spice products have reduced mainly due to increased supply, and there is no possibility of a price hike ahead of Eid-ul-Adha.

item.title: BCB ad-hoc committee initiates election process
item.description: To oversee the election, the ad-hoc committee has approved the formation of a three-member election commission.

item.title: Flood-affected farmers in haor region to receive BDT 7,500 initially: Agri Minister
item.description: The minister added that the government will provide support for three months, and the amount may be increased depending on the extent of damage.

item.title: No grounds to question ICT operations: Law Minister
item.description: The minister said that in his first formal meeting with the ICT prosecution team, they discussed how to address those problems through legal and administrative processes, and expressed hope that necessary steps will be taken in the future.

item.title: Iran says it turns back US warship from Strait of Hormuz, US official denies missile strike
item.description: Iran’s navy said it had prevented “American-Zionist” warships from entering ​the Strait of Hormuz area by issuing a “swift and decisive warning”.

item.title: Measles outbreak claims 17 children in deadliest single day
item.description: Of the 17 deaths, two were confirmed as measles; the remaining 15 children died while presenting measles-like symptoms.

item.title: PM directs simplifying project approval process to attract investment
item.description: The prime minister said the government is committed to creating an investment-friendly environment and emphasised the need to remove existing complexities in the investment process to achieve this goal.

item.title: Oil jumps by 5 percent after report of US warship being hit by missiles
item.description: Brent crude futures were up $5.52, or 5.1 percent, at $113.69 a barrel by 1025 GMT, having settled ​down $2.23 on Friday.

item.title: Bangladesh Bank to launch OVOP scheme targeting 10 million jobs
item.description: The “One Village One Product (OVOP)” plan proposes a shift from conventional collateral-based lending to value-chain financing that covers production, processing, logistics and export.

item.title: Agri dept says 71% paddy harvesting completed in Netrokona haor region
item.description: Although many farmers have managed to harvest their paddy with great effort, the crops are rotting in piles due to delays in drying and storage.

item.title: Highest spending on buffalo meat despite no approval
item.description: Industry insiders say that Bangladesh does not permit the import of beef, buffalo, or other meat products, except for limited quantities of premium frozen meat for five-star hotels. However, buffalo meat accounted for the highest share at approximately $577.8 million.

item.title: Govt targets doubling tourism GDP contribution: Tourism Minister
item.description: The minister said Bangladesh currently has 1,742 tourism spots spread across the country, indicating that every district has tourism potential.

item.title: US envoy leads Bangladesh team to investment summit in Washington
item.description: The delegation is participating in the SelectUSA Investment Summit being held from May 3 to 6 in Washington, DC.

item.title: Air-flow onion storage system fails to prevent rot in Meherpur
item.description: The technology was distributed in Mujibnagar upazila under an incentive scheme accompanied by official training, but farmers say stored bulbs are rotting within weeks.

item.title: US-led task force tells ships to reroute on first day of new effort to reopen the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The Joint Maritime Information Center said Monday that the U.S. had set up an “enhanced security area” south of typical shipping routes and urged mariners to coordinate closely with Omani authorities “due to anticipated high traffic volume.”

item.title: Eight river stations exceed danger levels in northeast Bangladesh
item.description: The Bangladesh Water Development Board said water levels had climbed further in most rivers in the region over the preceding 24 hours, with fresh pressure emerging at several points.

item.title: Bangladesh faces simultaneous slowdown in imports and exports
item.description: Trade analysts argue the EPB’s ten‑month export slide is more than a numerical setback; it reflects the global and domestic pressures bearing down on the economy.

item.title: China robot-hand-building unicorn Linkerbot targets $6 billion valuation
item.description: Linkerbot currently holds over 80 percent ​of the global market share in high-degree-of-freedom (DoF) robotic hands, and plans to scale production “soon” to 10,000 units a month from almost ⁠5,000 currently.

item.title: Indian High Commissioner pays farewell call on FM
item.description: The outgoing high commissioner reiterated his country’s intent to work closely with the people and the Government of Bangladesh to strengthen people-centric cooperation in all domains.

item.title: Three cruise ship passengers die in suspected hantavirus outbreak
item.description: Netherlands-based Oceanwide Expeditions said in a news release it was “managing a serious medical situation” on a polar expedition ship, the MV Hondius.

item.title: ADB unveils $70 billion plan for power grid, digital highway in Asia-Pacific
item.description: ADB said the shift from national-only grids to a networked regional market would mark a structural break in how Asia traded electricity.

item.title: Staff and drug shortages disrupt care at national burn institute
item.description: Approved posts for doctors, nurses and support workers remain heavily vacant, forcing the existing workforce to absorb the extra load. A prolonged shortage of medicines has deepened the strain.

item.title: Body of Bangladeshi PhD student killed in Florida returns to Dhaka
item.description: The 27-year-old was pursuing a PhD in geography, environmental science and policy at the University of South Florida.

item.title: Trump says US to help ships stranded in Strait of Hormuz as tanker hit by projectiles
item.description: Hundreds of ships and as many as 20,000 seafarers have been unable to transit the strait during the conflict, ​according to the International Maritime Organization.

item.title: Monira Sharmin writes to EC seeking halt to gazette on Nusrat before writ hearing
item.description: After hearing the writ petition, if the court directs acceptance of Monira’s nomination paper, voting will be held for the seat, allowing alliance-backed candidates to elect one of the two contenders, officials at the Election Commission said.

item.title: Remittance inflow crosses $3b mark in April
item.description: During this period, remittance receipts reached $3,127 million, marking a 13.6 percent increase year-on-year compared to $2,752 million in the same period last year.

item.title: Over 12.4 million animals available for Eid-ul-Adha: Livestock Minister
item.description: The minister noted that no import will be allowed from outside the country this year, like last year, and that’s why leasing of border hats remained suspended.

item.title: Crackdown intensified to curb crime in Dhaka: DMP
item.description: The acting commissioner said DMP’s goal is to dismantle criminal networks, restore public confidence and make Dhaka a safer city.

item.title: Inside Ukraine’s drive to defeat the dreaded Shahed drone
item.description: The pioneering low-cost, long-range attack drones ‌designed by Iran have indeed become the scourge of Ukraine, with Russia unleashing thousands of them on its enemy every month.

item.title: OPEC+ agrees third oil output quota hike since Hormuz closure
item.description: Seven OPEC+ countries will raise oil output targets by 188,000 barrels per day in June, the third consecutive ​monthly increase, OPEC+ said in a statement after an online meeting.

item.title: Anybody can speak in the current parliament freely: Moyeen Khan
item.description: The minister said that debate among political parties with differing opinions is a normal part of a democratic system.

item.title: Hezbollah pays steep price in battle to reverse its fortunes
item.description: In the Hezbollah-controlled southern suburbs of Beirut, more than two dozen freshly dug graves were quickly ​filled with fighters’ bodies in the days after the ceasefire took hold. Simple marble tombstones identify some as commanders, others as fighters.

item.title: Govt officials should have mentality to work in all positions: PM
item.description: The prime minister stated that his special request to DCs is to consider every path in public administration as important and inevitable.

item.title: Dhaka’s burial land crisis puts grave preservation beyond most families
item.description: Urban planners blame severe land scarcity. The two city corporations administer 251 acres of graveyard space across a combined 305.46 square kilometres, just 0.33 percent of the capital’s footprint.

item.title: Measles and its symptoms claim 10 more lives in 24 hours
item.description: During this period, measles’ symptoms were detected in 1,166 more children nationwide, the report said.

item.title: GolfHouse Diplomatic Cup 2026 concludes in Dhaka with diplomats, corporate leaders participating
item.description: Organisers said the event is part of a broader initiative to integrate golf into corporate culture and strengthen engagement between diplomats and business leaders through informal interaction, emphasising networking, discipline and leadership.

item.title: GolfHouse Diplomatic Cup 2026 concludes in Dhaka with diplomats, corporate leaders participating
item.description: Organisers said the event is part of a broader initiative to integrate golf into corporate culture and strengthen engagement between diplomats and business leaders through informal interaction, emphasising networking, discipline and leadership.

item.title: PM opens 4-day DC conference
item.description: Prime Minister Tarique Rahman inaugurated the annual DC Conference, where key governance challenges and 1,729 proposals were highlighted.

item.title: Less than 10% of Bangladesh’s Madrasah teachers formally trained
item.description: Stakeholders attribute this crisis primarily to the lack of adequate training centres for Madrasah teachers in the country.

item.title: Capital market financing equals just 6% of Bangladesh’s fixed capital in five decades
item.description: Relative to the size of the economy, no other nation’s capital market supplies so little finance. Adding to the distress is the fact that no new company has entered the market through an initial public offering in more than two years.

item.title: Mexican governor steps aside after US accusations of cartel ties
item.description: Rocha’s decision to step aside came as the U.S. has been exerting increasing pressure on Mexico to step up efforts to root out corruption and official collusion with organized ​crime.

item.title: Americans on a budget mourn loss of low-cost Spirit Airlines
item.description: Spirit abruptly cancelled flights overnight, stranding passengers and staff around the U.S., Caribbean and Latin America, after collapsing under financial pressures that included a sharp rise in fuel costs due to the Iran war.

item.title: Germany says US troop drawdown should spur Europe, but top Republicans worried
item.description: The Pentagon announced the drawdown from Germany on Friday, ‌as a rift over the Iran war and tariff tensions placed further strain on relations between the U.S. and Europe.

item.title: Trump says US could restart Iran strikes ‘if they misbehave’
item.description: Trump has said repeatedly that Iran can never ​have a nuclear weapon and said on Friday he was not satisfied with the latest Iranian proposal, while Iran’s foreign minister said Tehran was ready for diplomacy if the U.S. changes its ​approach.

item.title: Body of Bangladeshi PhD student killed in Tampa leaves for Dhaka
item.description: Representatives from the Consulate General of Bangladesh in Miami, including Consul General Sheheli Sabrin, oversaw the dispatch at the airport.

item.title: Info minister calls for balance between state and media
item.description: The minister argued that the definition of media and journalism had fundamentally shifted, and without balance, it would be difficult to keep pace with society, the economy and civilisation.

item.title: Judiciary gets less funding than state-run broadcaster, law minister says
item.description: The cramped funding envelope covered judges’ salaries and the administrative costs of every court, leaving almost nothing for the quality of justice delivery citizens expected, the minister added.

item.title: Home minister warns officers of action for negligence and misconduct
item.description: Stressing the importance of accountability, the minister said law enforcement must serve citizens with sincerity and fairness at all times.

item.title: PM inaugurates Basia river re-excavation in Sylhet
item.description: Addressing locals afterwards, the prime minister said the work would directly benefit 80,000 farmers and indirectly benefit another 150,000, while lifting crop production along its banks by an estimated 7,000 metric tonnes.

item.title: “Priority must go to dignified livelihood and safety for migrants abroad”
item.description: “We have to put human dignity first. Our human rights infrastructure needs to be strengthened. We’ve got to make sure our workers can earn a dignified living and stay safe while they’re abroad.”

item.title: Govt to start work soon to upgrade road, rail links on Dhaka–Sylhet route: PM
item.description: Noting that the concerned ministry has already taken initiatives to begin the construction work, he said, “I hope, InSha’Allah, it will be completed within two to three years”.

item.title: Mexico City is sinking so quickly, it can be seen from space
item.description: Mexico City has been sinking for more than a century, leaving many monuments and older buildings — like the Metropolitan Cathedral, where construction began in 1573 — visibly tilted to the side.

item.title: Iran standoff could leave Trump worse off than before he went to war
item.description: While there is little doubt that waves of U.S. and Israeli strikes heavily degraded Iran’s military capabilities, many of Trump’s often-shifting war objectives remain unfulfilled.

item.title: PM launches Surma riverfront beautification, flood protection project in Sylhet
item.description: During the event, officials briefed the Prime Minister on the details of the project.

item.title: Five years on, Dhaka’s two city corporations fail to revive a single canal
item.description: Experts note that the canals have merely changed hands and no real work followed. The priority after acquiring ownership was demarcation and restoration. Neither of the city corporations has fully restored a single canal since.

item.title: PM offers ziarat at Hazrat Shahjalal’s shrine
item.description: Earlier, the prime minister landed at Osmani International Airport at 10:05 am on a domestic flight.

item.title: Republican governors pursue new congressional maps after US Supreme Court ruling
item.description: The rapid developments underscored how the court’s decision, which severely weakened the landmark Voting Rights Act passed in 1965, has ​injected a fresh dose of chaos into what had already been a dizzying national fight over redistricting.

item.title: Dhaka to study potential monorail routes under revised transport blueprint
item.description: The scheme is cast as part of efforts to ease the city’s traffic congestion and modernise its mass transit. Monorail is seen as particularly suited to cramped, densely populated neighbourhoods.

item.title: US withdrawing 5,000 troops from Germany, US officials say
item.description: The Pentagon said the withdrawal was expected to be completed over the next six to 12 ​months.

item.title: President vows to ensure workers’ rights, dignity and safety
item.description: The President said that workers are the principal architects of the country’s development and the driving force of the economy.

item.title: PM joins workers’ rally at Naya Paltan
item.description: Prime Minister and BNP Chairman Tarique Rahman arrived at 4:15 pm and is due to address the gathering.

item.title: Law minister says forces that opposed Liberation War now legally defined
item.description: He said the definition of a freedom fighter under Section 2(10) encompasses those who fought for independence against the Pakistani military and its adjunct forces.

item.title: Golam Parwar insists Jamaat has moved beyond its 1971 legacy
item.description: Parwar insisted that the Jamaat of the 1971 era and today’s party “are not the same”, citing repeated overhauls of its constitution and policy framework.

item.title: Electric school bus exempt from all import duties, taxes: NBR
item.description: The move is expected to cut the cost of electric buses and accelerate the shift towards sustainable mobility in the education sector.

item.title: 40,590 Bangladeshi pilgrims reach Saudi Arabia
item.description: Subject to moon sighting, the holy Hajj is expected to be held in Saudi Arabia on May 26.

item.title: Govt prioritising workers’ welfare, safety and job creation: Mahdi Amin
item.description: Workers, the adviser said, were the key drivers of economic growth and prosperity, with industry, agriculture and infrastructure all depending on their tireless contributions.

item.title: Fuel crunch slows development works at Mirsharai economic zone
item.description: The fuel shortage has forced contractors to keep heavy machinery idle, delaying work across the site.

item.title: Trump administration says its war in Iran has been ‘terminated’ before 60-day deadline
item.description: The statement furthers an argument laid out by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth during testimony in the Senate earlier Thursday, when he said the ceasefire effectively paused the war.
